concerns.text: The plan assumes the GitHub Actions free-tier runner has FSL/AFNI pre-installed ('assumption: CI has FSL/AFNI'). This is factually incorrect; standard GitHub Actions runners (ubuntu-latest) do not include FSL or AFNI by default. Installing these heavy neuroimaging toolkits (often >2GB) and their dependencies within the 6-hour runtime limit, while also downloading and processing ~10GB of fMRI data, is infeasible. The plan must switch to a pure-Python stack (e.g., Nilearn for preprocessing) or a containerized environment that pre-bundles these tools, rather than relying on a non-existent pre-installed assumption.

The plan targets a 6-hour end-to-end execution on a 2 vCPU, 7GB RAM runner for 100+ participants. Preprocessing 100 rs-fMRI scans (motion correction, normalization, filtering) using Nilearn or similar Python libraries on 2 vCPUs will likely exceed this time limit significantly (often taking 10-20+ minutes per subject on CPU). The compute constraints (2 vCPU, 7GB RAM) are insufficient for the proposed volume of neuroimaging preprocessing within the stated timeframe. The plan needs to either reduce the sample size for the CI run, optimize the preprocessing pipeline (e.g., downsampling, parallelization strategies not feasible on 2 cores), or acknowledge the runtime violation.
